# Generated Code — Do Not Edit

This directory contains Rust code generated by `tonic-build` from the proto definitions in `daemon-bus/proto/`.

**Never edit files in this directory manually.**

## How code generation works

`build.rs` invokes `tonic-build` at compile time. The generated `.rs` files are written here
so that they can be checked into version control for CI caching and for IDE indexing without
requiring a build step.

To regenerate:

```sh
cargo build
```

Any file here that does not match the current proto definitions is stale. `cargo build` always
overwrites with the latest generated output.

## What lives here

After a successful build, you will see:

- `sena.daemonbus.v1.rs` — message types, service traits, and client/server stubs generated
  from `proto/sena.daemonbus.v1.proto`.

## Why this directory exists before the first build

The module declaration in `src/main.rs` references this path. The directory must exist (with at
least a valid Rust module file or an `include!` pointing to `OUT_DIR`) for the crate to compile.
On first build, `tonic-build` populates it. Until then, a stub module file is committed to keep
`cargo check` happy before the first full build.
